_comment: Phase 53 golden-file parity test fixture for the lessons distiller. The Phase 53 port (lessons-distiller as a bundled extension on ctx.llm + ctx.lessons) MUST produce the same lesson body when fed this conversation as the legacy host-side distiller does today. Diff is asserted character-by-character at distiller-parity.test.ts.

run_complete_event.finalContent: I'll fix the SQL injection by switching to a parameterized query.

recent_messages.content: Why is my login form returning a 500 error when the email contains an apostrophe?
That's a SQL injection vulnerability — the apostrophe is being interpreted as a string terminator in your query. Let me look at your auth code.
Yeah, I'm building the WHERE clause with string concatenation. I know it's bad.

Right — never concat user input into SQL. Use parameterized queries. Here's the fix:

```ts
const rows = await db.select().from(users).where(eq(users.email, email));
```

Drizzle handles escaping automatically. The tests should pass now and you've eliminated the SQLi class of bugs entirely.

expected_lesson_title: Use parameterized queries for user input
_phase_53_notes: When the bundled distiller extension lands, this fixture's `recent_messages` is fed to ctx.llm.complete() with the same DISTILLATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT the legacy distiller uses. The returned lesson must match `expected_lesson_slug` and the body must contain the parameterized-query guidance. Tolerance: title may vary in wording within +/-3 words; body must include the keyword 'parameterized'.
